findings.message: PII logged: owner_email (an email address) is written to application logs in the 'item_created' event. The model definition in db/models.py explicitly annotates owner_email as PII that must not be included in logs. Email addresses in logs persist PII in log storage beyond its purpose and expose it to anyone with log access.
findings.suggestion: Remove owner_email from the log call. Log only non-PII identifiers (e.g. log.info("item_created", item_id=item.id)). If owner attribution is needed for auditing, log a non-reversible reference or a hashed/redacted form rather than the raw address.

findings.message: Unnecessary PII collection: ItemCreate accepts owner_email from the client, but create_item(session, payload.name) never persists it (the repository function ignores owner_email entirely). The email is collected only to be logged, which is not a legitimate stated purpose. Collecting PII that is not stored/used for the feature violates data-minimization.
findings.suggestion: Either remove owner_email from ItemCreate if it is not needed, or thread it through to create_item so it is stored for the stated purpose instead of merely being logged.
